title: Multimodal dataset from the CMx7-MM Experiment
description: A multimodal neurophysiological dataset acquired from 30 healthy adults performing a hierarchy of cognitive, motor, and combined cognitive-motor tasks. The dataset includes concurrent EEG, functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS), electrocardiography, electromyography, and behavioral measures across three sessions. This resource enables investigation of brain-behavior relationships during isolated and integrated cognitive-motor processing.
methods_description: Data were collected from 30 right-handed healthy adults (12 males, 18 females) across three sessions of approximately 30 minutes each. Participants performed seven tasks in randomized order: mental arithmetic, 2-back auditory task, motor imagery, passive motor movement (Biodex-driven), active motor movement, combined N-back arithmetic, and full cognitive-motor task. EEG was recorded using 32 channels in the 10-20 system; fNIRS covered prefrontal and sensorimotor regions. Physiological measures included ECG (Delsys Trigno) and EMG. Behavioral responses were captured via push-button and Biodex torque/kinematic measures. Subjective assessments included Karolinska Sleepiness Scale and cognitive load ratings. All recordings are raw exports without offline preprocessing.
